title: The Wellness Reset: A Two-Night Peninsula Weekend
dek: Thermal pools, a long walk, a slow lunch, and two nights of actual sleep. For people whose weekend brief is simply to stop.

stops.note: Check in early enough to stretch the garden walk before the light goes. Keep the pace soft from minute one.
Dinner should be unfussy on night one. The Merricks General kitchen handles this brief without theatre.
Book a morning session. Move through the circuit properly instead of ticking pools off a list.
Lunch on the vineyard terrace if the weather allows. Keep it lighter than a degustation.
A short cliff walk resets the nervous system between lunch and dinner without overcommitting.
Second night at the same property. Skip dinner out and let the in-house kitchen deliver the softer version.
On the way home, stop at the regional gallery for a quiet hour before the drive back.

editorNote: This is the itinerary to use when the weekend is genuinely meant to recover something rather than impress anyone. The geography deliberately stays tight — one stay, one thermal session, two gentle meals, one short walk, one gallery on the way out — and the bookings are deliberately easy to cancel if the day turns bad. The whole plan is built to let the Peninsula do its best-known trick: slow people down.
